1989_05_27-USA-New_Vrindaban-SB_7_9_40-Krishna_Is_Our_Only_Shelter-st2-vr02

Meta Data v9

Event Name/Title

Krishna Is Our Only Shelter

Event Date

27 May 1989

Event Location

USA, New Vrindaban

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Lakshmi Vijaya devi dasi

Completed

17 Nov 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-007-Krsna%20is%20Our%20Only%20Shelter%20(NV)-SB_7_9_40+FN-dt_89_05_27.mp3

Source Video

NA

Verse

SB 7.9.40

Tags – Places

Himalayas, Ganga Sagar

Tags – Pastimes

Krishna eliminating forest fire

Tags – Persons

Krishna, Prahlad, Narada, Maya, Bhakthivinod Thakur, Srila Prabhupada, Ganga

Tags – Themes

Controlling senses, prajalpa, controlling tongue, spiritual master, guru, devotional service, spiritual life

jihvaikato ’cyuta vikarṣati māvitṛptā
śiśno ’nyatas tvag-udaraṁ śravaṇaṁ kutaścit
ghrāṇo ’nyataś capala-dṛk kva ca karma-śaktir
bahvyaḥ sapatnya iva geha-patiṁ lunanti

[SB 7.9.40]

Translation

My dear Lord, O infallible one, my position is like that of a person who has many wives, all trying to attract him in their own way. For example, the tongue is attracted to palatable dishes, the genitals to sex with an attractive woman, and the sense of touch to contact with soft things. The belly, although filled, still wants to eat more, and the ear, not attempting to hear about You, is generally attracted to cinema songs. The sense of smell is attracted to yet another side, the restless eyes are attracted to scenes of sense gratification, and the active senses are attracted elsewhere. In this way I am certainly embarrassed.

Purport

The human form of life is meant for God realization, but this process, which begins with śravaṇaṁ kīrtanaṁ viṣṇoḥ — hearing and chanting of the holy name of the Lord — is disturbed as long as our senses are materially attracted. Therefore, devotional service means purifying the senses. In the conditioned state our senses are covered by material sense gratification, and as long as one is not trained in purifying the senses, one cannot become a devotee. In our Kṛṣṇa Consciousness movement, therefore, we advise from the very beginning that one restrict the activities of the senses, especially the tongue, which is described by Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura as most greedy and unconquerable. To stop this attraction of the tongue, one is authoritatively advised not to accept meat or similar uneatable things nor to allow the tongue to hanker to drink or smoke. Even the drinking of tea and coffee is not permitted. Similarly, the genitals must be restricted from illicit sex. Without such restraint of the senses, one cannot make advancement in Kṛṣṇa Consciousness. The only method of controlling the senses is to chant and hear the holy name of the Lord; otherwise, one will always be disturbed, as a householder with more than one wife would be disturbed by them for sense gratification.

Lecture Transcription

Prayers of Prahlad Maharaj

This morning, we are continuing our reading of the prayers offered by Prahlad Maharaj to the Supreme Personality of Godhead in his munificent form of Lord Narasimha Dev. It is said,

mūkaṁ karoti vācālaṁ
paṅguṁ laṅghayate girim
yat-kṛpā tam ahaṁ vande
śrī-guruṁ dīna-tāraṇam

[CC Madhya 17.80]

Translation

[‘The Supreme Personality of Godhead has the form of sac-cid-ānanda-vigraha — transcendental bliss, knowledge and eternity. I offer my respectful obeisances unto Him, who turns the dumb into eloquent speakers and enables the lame to cross mountains. Such is the mercy of the Lord.’]

that by the mercy of Krishna and the spiritual master, even the most unqualified becomes qualified. Here Prahlad was only five years old, who had never studied the Vedas, who had never studied elevated philosophy of life, he is speaking eloquently, the highest most consistent undefeatable philosophy ever to be heard in the history of the world. How is this possible?

While in the womb of his mother in the most helpless and apparently unqualified material condition he was given the blessing of his spiritual master, Srila Narada Muni and simply on the basis of that blessing, hearing from his guru submissively with a desire to serve, aah… Prahlad Maharaj here is performing what is humanly impossible aah… and factually the path of Bhakti is based on this principle. It is said in Bhagavad Gita,

rāga-dveṣa-vimuktais tu
viṣayān indriyaiś caran
ātma-vaśyair vidheyātmā
prasādam adhigacchati

[BG-2.64]

[One who can control his senses by practicing the regulated principles of freedom can obtain the complete mercy of the Lord and thus become free from all attachment and aversion.]

that one who follows the principles of devotion to the lord under the guidance of the Spiritual Master, he attains the complete mercy of the Lord and thus he is free from all attraction and aversion. So, simply by attaining the mercy of the lord we can overcome all obstacles, otherwise this material nature is an impossible, unconquerable impediment in our spiritual lives, without the mercy of the lord, without surrendering to the lord.

There is an example that the mighty river flows, aah… the Ganges flows from the peaks of the Himalayas and it is constantly flowing without hindrance to Ganga Sagar, which is aah… the Indian Ocean, Bay of Bengal. Now sometimes, big powerful mighty elephants, the most powerful beings on earth, they go into the river and because of the powerful waves with all their strength, with all their huge size they are washed away. Why? Because all though, they're the most powerful beings on earth, when they try to fight the current of the riv…river, they are in hopeless condition and then there's the tiniest smallest of fish, that tiniest smallest of fish in the river can swim across the upstream downstream effortlessly. Why is that? What is the difference in strength between an elephant and a fish, but the elephant is defeated, and the fish is free! Simply because, the elephant is depending on his own strength but against the river his strength is infinitely small but the tiny little fish, meek and helpless as it is, has learned how to take shelter of the river, therefore free.

Krishna is the only shelter

So, Krishna says in Bhagavad-Gita, “daivī hy eṣā guṇa-mayī mama māyā duratyayā [BG-7.14], that ‘this material nature consisting of the three modes of material nature is undefeatable, it's very difficult to overcome, but one who surrenders to me, one who takes shelter of me, with humility and devotion he can easily cross over it.’ Sometimes it is said that this material existence is like a blazing fire, “saṁsāra-dāvānala-līḍha-loka” [Sri Gurvastakam 1] it is like a blazing fire and Maya is constantly trying to allure us into the heart of this blazing fire. She does not reveal to you the nature of this fire until you are completely trapped in her aah…phenomenal existence. She makes it appear to be very nice place.

There's a story in the Srimad-Bhagavatam, how, when Lord Krishna was herding the cows in Sri Vrindavan, there were beautiful fresh delicious grasses, they were attracting the cows and the calves aah…and being attracted by these grasses the cows and calves were going deeper and deeper and deeper into the forest and Krishna and his cowherd boys, they were wondering “Ah! Where…where have they gone? Where is [are] our cows?” so they've gone deeper deeper into the forest to search for them and all of a sudden when they found them, ah, a blazing fire began to roar. This fire, its flames were literally reaching the sky and it surrounded them on all four sides. They were just little boys, helpless cows! See this is Maya's trick, she allures you. As devotees the first thing she'll try to do is allure you through sense gratification, away from the protection of Krishna aah…with nice apparent fresh grasses. Here is some nice money, here is some nice man or woman, here is some nice position, here is a nice comfortable situation, she's alluring us with these fresh grasses and then aah…before we know it, we find ourselves in the middle of an unquenchable fire, which is simply meant to devour us. Aah…So, in that condition, the cows and the cowherd boys they had nothing to do except just turn to Krishna. It is said that they turned to Krishna as a dying man looks at the…a picture of The Supreme Personality of Godhead and with heart full of devotion they took shelter of his lotus feet. ‘Krishna please save us otherwise we are finished’ and at that time Krishna, casting his merciful glance upon them, he opened his mouth and inhaled and when he inhaled, the whole fire was devoured aah…and in this way, he protected them. factually, this is the situation of every one of us. We are all in that fire and we are all about to be consumed by the force of time, Krishna is devouring everything like a raging fire. But if we turn to Krishna with that helpless dependence of the cowherd boys, then we too can easily be saved from this burning fire of material existence.

Maya is very powerful

But Maya, she is very clever and she's very powerful. One-time Srila Prabhupad said that Maya was as powerful as Krishna, because she is Krishna's energy, “mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛtiḥ, sūyate sa-carācaram” [BG 9.10] but she is working under his direction. So, devotional service, spiritual life is like a battle against Maya, and you have to understand the strengths of your enemy and you have to understand the weaknesses of your enemy and believe me, Maya knows our strengths and she knows our weaknesses, inside and out from top to bottom. She knows everything about you, there's nothing she does not know, aah… and she knows just where you're vulnerable and that is where she will strike you. It's like in the boxing arena, the two opponents are smashing each other with their fists ah…and as soon as they see some blood they realize “Ah! he's vulnerable right there!” and practically every punch from then on is right toward that area where it's bleeding, knowing that when something is vulnerable you just zero in centre on that, concentrate on that and soon your opponent is finished. Aah…So Maya is like this, she knows exactly where we are vulnerable where we are weak. Sometimes we talk about coming in like a needle and out like a plough. Aah… Prabhupad used to tell us in preaching, “sometimes you have to go in like a needle and out like a plough.” Aah… You come in very subtle and gentle and then you go out [maharaja making sound] and destroy all the material conceptions and make Krishna as Supreme. Somehow or other Maya also knows this technique. Aah… She will come in like a needle and if you let her in, before too long you will find that she has come out like a plough. Aah…Maya, the illusory energy of the lord is like a thief trying to break into your house and you have many treasures in your house and that treasure is your devotion to Krishna, your faith in Krishna and Maya is constantly trying to steal it and your senses are like the windows and doors. If you leave any one of them open, that thief will somehow or other sneak in and steal everything away. Aah…For the gross materialist they just leave their front door wide open and go out right? and Maya, the robber, the thief, she just walks in, takes everything away and walks out, no problem. Right! But for devotee, it's not so easy. For a devotee sometimes Maya has to find some little backdoor or some little window and creep in and hide in the basement aah…and wait till you're just sleeping or inattentive and then that very secretly, you don't even know she's there, but you wake up one morning and everything's gone. Aah…Maya is grosser than the grossest and subtler than the subtlest and she knows exactly how to get you, of this, there is no doubt. Maya only has one weakness, we have hundreds of millions of weaknesses, but Maya only has one weakness and that is, that she is also surrendered to Krishna and when Krishna is present within our heart, when Krishna is present within our mind, she cannot enter. So that is our only hope.

Intelligence is carried away by senses

Here Prahlad Maharaj is describing the position of a person, who is attached to his sense gratification, that he's constantly being dragged by the illusory energy in the form of the objects of the senses. Krishna says in Gita,

indriyāṇāṁ hi caratāṁ
yan mano 'nuvidhīyate
tad asya harati prajṣāṁ
vāyur nāvam ivāmbhasi

[BG -2.67]

[As a boat on the water is swept away by a strong wind, even one of the senses on which the mind focuses can carry away a man's intelligence.]

as a boat on the river is swept away by strong winds even one of the senses on which the mind focuses will carry away a man's intelligence. It is described that, there are various animals, and each particular specie of life has a particular type of sense gratification that they're very attracted and therefore a hunter knows exactly how to catch that particular animal by simply tempting them with that particular type of sense gratification. It is said that the moth is very attracted to see beautiful things. Sometimes when it is in the summer at night, the window is closed and the light is on, the moth is so attracted about light that he will just do anything to get in the room. Sometime that light is a candle, a burning fire and the moth, he's just banging against the window going back and flying as hard as he could, into the window trying to breakthrough it to get to that light that fire, do anything, so many austerities, smashing against the window and somehow or other when he gets through. Aah! it is his great victory. It is great pleasure to just dive into the fire only to find that he is burnt to ashes. So, the moth is destroyed simply by the influence of that sense, that is most finding pleasure.

Similarly, the method of catching elephants, how do you catch an elephant? They're so powerful. In the old days they didn't have those big elephant guns. How does a little man catch an elephant? Very simple... elephants are very attracted to sex life. This particular sense is very strong so what they do is, they take a female elephant and put her next to a big ditch in the ground and the male elephant sees this female elephant and thinks, ah, how nice the sense of touch and he begins to try to have nice pleasure with this female elephant and then she is trying to push him into the ditch, and he's helplessly trapped.

The deer is very attracted to sweet music, so the hunter plays the flute, and the deer is, although they are well hidden in the forest, they come right before the hunter, all need to be trapped and killed by their desire to find pleasure in hearing, they are conquered. And it is said that the bee is very attracted to the sense of smell, so the bee goes into certain types of flower that smells very sweet and then that flower closes around them and traps them. You can never get out.

And the fish has a very uncontrollable tongue aah…and if you want to catch a fish you just get little… nice little worm or a smaller type of fish and when the fish see that ah! Thinking how…how delicious! and the fish goes running chasing after the bait and by the influence of his uncontrolled tongue, he's caught by the hook of the fisherman and trapped.

So, in this way if we allow any one of our five senses or the mind to bait us for its sense gratification, Maya will trap us. She will conquer us; it is just a matter of time.

Senses must be engaged in spiritual activities

Therefore, a devotee of the Lord must always be very careful and very strict to constantly keep the senses engaged in positive spiritual activities.

sarvopādhi-vinirmuktaṁ
tat-paratvena nirmalam
hṛṣīkeṇa hṛṣīkeśa-
sevanaṁ bhaktir ucyate

[CC Madhya 19.170]

that if the senses are constantly engaged in the service of the lord of the senses, aah…then they become purified rather than becoming entangled or bondage. Krishna says in the Bhagavad-Gita, work done as sacrifice for Vishnu must be performed, otherwise any activity, any thought that you perform in your life will only entangle you further in the web of bondage and suffering and illusion. Therefore, the duty of the sadhu and the guru is to make sure that the disciples are fully engaged in Krishna’s service, because any sense that is not engaged in Krishna’s service will inevitably be carried away by Maya because we are pleasure-seeking entities.

Tongue is the most uncontrollable of all senses

And as Bhaktivinoda Thakur has sung, the most difficult of all the senses to control is the tongue. It is said if you can control the tongue, automatically all the other senses will be under your control and if you cannot control your tongue, it is utterly impossible to control any of the other senses. So how to control the tongue? The tongue is very greedy, Jibe…aah…Bhaktivinoda Thakur says, the tongue is very greedy, lobha. Therefore, it's two functions of tasting and vibrating must constantly be filled with Krishna. We eat only spiritually sanctified foodstuffs, not too much and not too little, in this way the sense of taste is fully under control, and we never talk mundane subject matters which are not conducive to our spiritual advancement. This is actually the utterly worst that a devotee can do, prajalpa! Rupa Goswami describes, this discussing prajalpa or non-spiritual subject matters is the basic principle for falling down, because mundane subjects completely strip away from us the propensity for service to God and it is impossible for anyone speaking nonsense subject matters to control his other senses for any period of time. It is not possible. Srila Prabhupad would sometimes tell us that when someone talks all nonsense, sometimes his guru makes him follow a vow of maun or silence that ‘don't say anything for the rest of your life.’ In India there are such Mauni babas, they have little, usually, they don't have any money, they find a little piece of a blackboard with jagged edges, find a piece of chalk. If they want to talk, they just start writing, usually all nonsense, but they're not allowed to talk.

But actually the perfection of silence is to vibrate the pure sound of the name and glories of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and if we can always keep our tongue filled with the praise of the lord and his devotees aah…and we can understand, by the mercy of God, all our other senses will naturally be under control and this is the basic principle of spiritual life in this age of Kali, to keep our tongue filled with the nectar of the holy name of God. It is described in the Bṛhan Naradiya Puraṇa,

“harer nāma harer nāma harer nāmaiva kevalam

kalau nāsty eva nāsty eva nāsty eva gatir anyathā.”

[Brhan-Naradiya Purana 38.126], [CC Adi 17.21]

that in this age of Kali, the process is always keeping our tongue filled with the holy name of Krishna is the only means of purification and deliverance. So, aah…it is a very simple formula, it only requires a very sincere and earnest endeavor. Krishna says in the Gita, that for his devotee he preserves what he has and carries what he lacks. If we simply endeavor in this way, by keeping saintly association and always hearing and chanting the glories of the lord with great determination, that it is certain, by the mercy of the lord we will overcome all of the hundreds and trillions of obstacles that Maya puts before us in our life, and we can attain the supreme destination of pure devotion unto the lotus feet of Shri Krishna. Thank you very much!
